Package org.eventb.core

Interface IPSStatus

All Superinterfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IElementManipulation, IInternalElement, IParent, IPOStampedElement, IPRProofInfoElement, IRodinElement, ISnapshotable
All Known Implementing Classes:
PSStatus
public interface IPSStatus extends IInternalElement, IPRProofInfoElement, IPOStampedElement
Common protocol for Proof Status elements in Event-B Proof Status (PS) files.

The convention used for associating proof obligations (IPOSequent) in the PO file to proof status elements (IPSStatus) in the PS file, and proofs (in the PR file) is that they all have the identical element name.

Some attributes in this internal element (i.e. confidence and hasManualProof) are cached versions of the corresponding proof in case it exists. Clients writing into the PSFile must make sure that they are identical to the attributes stored in the corresponding proof.

Clients shall not modify directly proof statuses through this interface, but rather use the higher level Proof Manager API (see IProofManager) which ensures that proof obligations, proofs and proof statuses are kept in synch.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • , IPOSequent, IPRProof, IProofManager

  • Field Details

    • ELEMENT_TYPE

      static final IInternalElementType<IPSStatus> ELEMENT_TYPE

  • Method Details

    • getProof

      IPRProof getProof()
      Returns the proof associated to this proof obligation from the RODIN database.

      This is a handle-only method. The proof element may or may not be present.

      Returns:
      the proof associated to this status element

    • getPOSequent

      IPOSequent getPOSequent()
      Returns the proof obligation associated to this status element from the RODIN database.

      This is a handle-only method. The returned element may or may not be present.

      Returns:
      the sequent associated to this proof obligation

    • isBroken

      boolean isBroken() throws RodinDBException
      Returns whether this proof obligation has a broken proof, that is a proof that does not match its sequent, and thus can't be used to discharge the proof obligation.

      The returned value is true iff the corresponding attribute contains true. Hence, if the attribute is absent, false is returned.

      Returns:
      true if the associated proof is broken
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • setBroken(boolean, IProgressMonitor)

    • setBroken

      void setBroken(boolean value, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets whether this proof obligation has a broken proof.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to set to
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • isBroken()

    • getConfidence

      int getConfidence() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the confidence associated with this proof obligation. The confidence is stored in an attribute which contains a local copy of the confidence attribute of the associated proof. If there is no associated proof yet, then the confidence attribute is not set and this method returns IConfidence.UNATTEMPTED.

      Important Note: The returned value is meaningless if the broken attribute (as returned by method isBroken()) is true. In that case, clients should consider that the confidence is unknown.

      This is a cached attribute in case there is an associated proof.

      Specified by:
      getConfidence in interface IPRProofInfoElement
      Returns:
      the confidence associated to this proof obligation
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • getProof()
      • isBroken()
      • copyProofInfo(IProgressMonitor)

    • getHasManualProof

      boolean getHasManualProof() throws RodinDBException
      Returns whether this proof obligation has been discharged manually. A proof obligation is considered as manually discharged if the end user entered manually its associated proof (even partially).

      The returned value is true iff the corresponding attribute contains true. Hence, if the attribute is absent, false is returned.

      This is a cached attribute in case there is an associated proof.

      Specified by:
      getHasManualProof in interface IPRProofInfoElement
      Returns:
      true if the user contributed to the proof of this proof obligation
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • copyProofInfo

      void copyProofInfo(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Copies the cached proof information attributes described in IPRProofInfoElement from the associated proof in case it exists.

      In case the associated proof does not exist, all proof information attributes are removed.

      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • IPRProofInfoElement

    • isContextDependent

      boolean isContextDependent() throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      Since:
      3.0

      value -
      monitor -
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      Since:
      3.0
